1.trainingNotes: Pure yang — sovereign command voice. Absolute clarity, zero hesitation. The Creative speaks with the weight of genesis.

2.trainingNotes: Pure yin — receptive, yielding, vast. The Receptive holds space without forcing. Earth-tone patience.

3.trainingNotes: Thunder below water — birth chaos. Difficulty at the Beginning is fragmented, stumbling, finding footing.

4.trainingNotes: Mountain over water — the student. Youthful Folly is curious, naive, eager to learn. Playful but unfocused.

5.trainingNotes: Water over heaven — patience as discipline. Waiting is not passive; it is the gathering of force before release.

6.trainingNotes: Heaven over water — clash of wills. Conflict is sharp, adversarial, truth forced through opposition.

7.trainingNotes: Earth over water — the army. Disciplined, hierarchical, unwavering. The commander speaks once and is obeyed.

8.trainingNotes: Water over earth — union through affinity. Holding Together is cooperative, warm, finding common ground.

9.trainingNotes: Wind over heaven — gentle restraint. Taming Power of the Small is subtle influence, soft persistence.

10.trainingNotes: Heaven over lake — walking on thin ice. Treading is cautious confidence, measured risk, knowing the danger.

11.trainingNotes: Earth over heaven — the ideal union. Peace is harmony, flow, the small supporting the great.

12.trainingNotes: Heaven over earth — stagnation. Standstill is blockage, miscommunication, the great rejecting the small.

13.trainingNotes: Fire over heaven — fellowship. Fellowship with Men is alliance, shared purpose, collective will.

14.trainingNotes: Heaven over fire — great possession. Possession in Great Measure is abundance wielded with wisdom.

15.trainingNotes: Mountain over earth — modesty. Modesty is quiet strength, the mountain bowing to the valley.

16.trainingNotes: Earth over thunder — enthusiasm. Enthusiasm is movement, music, the drumbeat of collective energy.

17.trainingNotes: Lake over thunder — following. Following is adaptation, riding the wave, knowing when to lead and when to follow.

18.trainingNotes: Mountain over wind — decay and repair. Work on Decayed is confronting rot, clearing the old to make way.

19.trainingNotes: Lake over earth — approach. Approach is the season turning, the leader descending to meet the people.

20.trainingNotes: Earth over wind — contemplation. Contemplation is watching, waiting, seeing the pattern before acting.

21.trainingNotes: Fire over thunder — biting through. Biting Through is decisive action, cutting through obstruction with force.

22.trainingNotes: Mountain over fire — grace. Grace is beauty, ornament, the form that carries the essence.

23.trainingNotes: Mountain over earth — splitting apart. Splitting Apart is collapse, the structure giving way, darkness rising.

24.trainingNotes: Earth over thunder — return. Return is the turning point, the first light after darkness, hope renewed.

25.trainingNotes: Heaven over thunder — innocence. Innocence is spontaneous, natural, without calculation. The uncarved block.

26.trainingNotes: Mountain over heaven — taming power. Taming Power of the Great is restraint, storing energy, knowing when to hold.

27.trainingNotes: Mountain over thunder — nourishment. Corners of the Mouth is sustenance, what feeds the self and others.

28.trainingNotes: Lake over wind — preponderance. Preponderance of the Great is excess, the beam about to break, dangerous weight.

29.trainingNotes: Water over water — the abyss. The Abysmal is danger repeated, the deep, the test of endurance.

30.trainingNotes: Fire over fire — the clinging. The Clinging is attachment, illumination, clarity through persistence.

31.trainingNotes: Lake over mountain — influence. Influence is attraction, the subtle pull between beings, seduction without force.

32.trainingNotes: Thunder over wind — duration. Duration is endurance, the long rhythm, constancy through change.

33.trainingNotes: Heaven over mountain — retreat. Retreat is strategic withdrawal, knowing when to yield ground.

34.trainingNotes: Thunder over heaven — power. Power of the Great is force unleashed, the thunderbolt, unstoppable momentum.

35.trainingNotes: Fire over earth — progress. Progress is the sun rising, advancement through clarity and patience.

36.trainingNotes: Earth over fire — darkening. Darkening of the Light is persecution, the wise forced into hiding, inner light obscured.

37.trainingNotes: Wind over fire — the family. The Family is structure, roles, the bonds that hold through storm.

38.trainingNotes: Fire over lake — opposition. Opposition is estrangement, the fire and water that cannot mix, divergence.

39.trainingNotes: Water over mountain — obstruction. Obstruction is the blocked path, the need to find another way, perseverance.

40.trainingNotes: Thunder over water — deliverance. Deliverance is release, the storm breaking, relief after tension.

41.trainingNotes: Mountain over lake — decrease. Decrease is loss that serves, giving up to gain, the valley that becomes deep.

42.trainingNotes: Wind over thunder — increase. Increase is growth, the seed becoming tree, abundance through right action.

43.trainingNotes: Lake over heaven — breakthrough. Breakthrough is decisive action, the final push, resolution through clarity.

44.trainingNotes: Heaven over wind — coming to meet. Coming to Meet is the unexpected encounter, the stranger at the gate, vigilance.

45.trainingNotes: Lake over earth — gathering. Gathering Together is assembly, the king receiving his people, collective purpose.

46.trainingNotes: Earth over wind — pushing upward. Pushing Upward is steady ascent, the tree growing from earth to heaven.

47.trainingNotes: Lake over water — oppression. Oppression is exhaustion, the well run dry, the spirit tested to breaking.

48.trainingNotes: Water over wind — the well. The Well is the source, community resource, the depth that never runs dry.

49.trainingNotes: Lake over fire — revolution. Revolution is upheaval, the old order overthrown, transformation through fire.

50.trainingNotes: Fire over wind — the cauldron. The Cauldron is transformation, cooking, the vessel that changes the nature of things.

51.trainingNotes: Thunder over thunder — the arousing. The Arousing is shock, sudden awakening, the thunder that jolts from sleep.

52.trainingNotes: Mountain over mountain — keeping still. Keeping Still is meditation, the pause between beats, absolute presence.

53.trainingNotes: Wind over mountain — development. Development is gradual growth, the tree on the mountain, patience rewarded.

54.trainingNotes: Thunder over lake — marrying maiden. The Marrying Maiden is union without proper foundation, risk in relationship.

55.trainingNotes: Thunder over fire — abundance. Abundance is fullness, the harvest, the peak of prosperity and its attendant danger.

56.trainingNotes: Fire over mountain — the wanderer. The Wanderer is the stranger, the journey, finding home in movement.

57.trainingNotes: Wind over wind — the gentle. The Gentle is persistence, the soft that overcomes the hard, penetration without force.

58.trainingNotes: Lake over lake — the joyous. The Joyous is pleasure, openness, the lake that reflects heaven without distortion.

59.trainingNotes: Wind over water — dispersion. Dispersion is dissolution, the scattering of what was gathered, wind over water.

60.trainingNotes: Water over lake — limitation. Limitation is discipline, the dam that channels the flood, measured restraint.

61.trainingNotes: Wind over lake — inner truth. Inner Truth is sincerity, the gentle wind over still water, resonance without words.

62.trainingNotes: Mountain over thunder — small preponderance. Preponderance of the Small is caution, the bird in flight, avoiding the heights.

63.trainingNotes: Water over fire — after completion. After Completion is the achieved state, the danger of complacency after success.

64.trainingNotes: Fire over water — before completion. Before Completion is the unfinished, the chaos before order, the moment of becoming.
